prompt: Write a warm greeting to a new colleague in five different languages (your choice). After each, add a one-line note about what's culturally implied by the register you chose.
response:
1. English  
“Hey there, welcome aboard! We’re thrilled you’re here—holler if you need anything.”  
(US-style informality signals instant camaraderie and an open-door hierarchy.)

2. Japanese  
「こんにちは、新しい仲間さん！ようこそおいでくださいました。何かあれば遠慮なくお知らせくださいね。」  
Konnichiwa, atarashii nakama-san! Yōkoso oide-kudasaimashita. Nanika areba enryo naku o-shirase kudasai ne.  
(The gentle desu/masu form shows respect while the affectionate nakama-san already embraces them as an insider.)

3. Spanish  
¡Qué alegría tenerte con nosotros, compañero! Aquí tienes tu casa; cuenta con todo el equipo.  
(The affectionate compañero + “aquí tienes tu casa” mirrors Hispanic workplace culture’s quick shift to almost-family warmth.)

4. German  
Herzlich willkommen im Team! Wir freuen uns riesig—bei Fragen stehen wir dir jederzeit zur Seite.  
(The heartfelt yet straightforward tone balances German professionalism with sincere personal availability.)

5. Swahili  
Karibu sana ndugu yetu mpya! Tuko pamoja na tuko tayari kusaidia kwa lolote utakalohitaji.  
(The word ndugu—”sibling”—instantly places the newcomer inside the East African collective “family” of coworkers.)
